title: Determining a context-dependent virtual distance using measurements of stigmergic interference
abstract: Systems and methods are disclosed for determining a context-dependent virtual distance based on stigmergic interference. The method may include obtaining environmental status information relating to an environment in proximity to a client device, calculating, based on the obtained environmental status information, the context-dependent virtual distance between the client device and a user of the client device, and controlling a user signaling pattern of the client device based on the calculated context-dependent virtual distance.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A method for determining a context-dependent virtual distance based on stigmergic interference, comprising: obtaining environmental status information relating to an environment in proximity to a client device; calculating, based on the obtained environmental status information, the context- dependent virtual distance between the client device and a user of the client device; and controlling a user signaling pattern of the client device based on the calculated context-dependent virtual distance. 2. The method of claim 1, further comprising estimating a physical proximity between the user and the client device, wherein calculating the context-dependent virtual distance comprises calculating the context-dependent virtual distance based on both the obtained environmental status information and the estimated physical proximity. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein: estimating the physical proximity comprises estimating a first physical proximity between a first user and a first client device, and the method further comprises estimating a second physical proximity between a second user and a second client device within the environment, wherein the first physical proximity and the second physical proximity are substantially equal; and calculating the context-dependent virtual distance comprises calculating a first virtual distance based on both the obtained environmental status information and the estimated first physical proximity, and the method further comprises calculating a second virtual distance based on both the obtained environmental status information and the estimated second physical proximity, wherein the first virtual distance and the second virtual distance are substantially different. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein calculating the context-dependent virtual distance further comprises calculating the context-dependent virtual distance based on a client device type of the client device. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein: obtaining the environmental status information comprises obtaining the environmental status information from one or more contextual devices; and calculating the context-dependent virtual distance further comprises calculating the context-dependent virtual distance based on a contextual device type of the one or more contextual devices. 6. The method of claim 1, further comprising: predicting, based on the calculated context-dependent virtual distance, an answer reaction time of the user in response to a first user signaling pattern of the client device, and wherein controlling the user signaling pattern comprises controlling the user signaling pattern based on the predicted answer reaction time. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein: wherein the calculated context-dependent virtual distance is increased based on an expectation that it will be difficult for the user to access the client device, and wherein the predicted answer reaction time is increased based on the increase of the calculated context-dependent virtual distance. 8. The method of claim 6, wherein: wherein the calculated context-dependent virtual distance is increased based on an expectation that the user will travel indirectly to the client device to answer a given communication with a given communication type, and wherein the predicted answer reaction time is increased based on the increase of the calculated context-dependent virtual distance. 9. The method of claim 6, wherein: 

 wherein the calculated context-dependent virtual distance with respect to an interactive video and/or audio interactions is increased relative to the calculated context- dependent virtual distance with respect to non-interactive video, audio, and/or textual interactions, wherein the predicted answer reaction time with respect to the interactive video and/or audio interactions is increased relative to the predicted answer reaction time with respect to the non-interactive video, audio, and/or textual interactions. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein the method is performed in the client device, in another client device, or in a remote device. 1 1. The method of claim 1, wherein the environment is an Internet of Things (IoT) environment. 12. The method of claim 1, further comprising: tracking at least one of (i) interactions between the user and the client device, (ii) changes in the environmental status information, (iii) changes in an estimated physical proximity between the user and the client device, or (iv) a combination thereof; wherein calculating the context-dependent virtual distance further comprises calculating the context-dependent virtual distance based on the at least one of (i) the interactions between the user and the client device, (ii) changes in the environmental status information, (iii) changes in the estimated physical proximity between the user and the client device, or (iv) the combination thereof. 13. The method of claim 1, further comprising: adjusting a power usage pattern based on the calculated context-dependent virtual distance. 14. A hub device for determining a context-dependent virtual distance based on stigmergic interference, comprising: a transceiver configured to obtain environmental status information relating to an environment in proximity to a client device; a processor configured to: 

 calculate, based on the obtained environmental status information, a context-dependent virtual distance between a user and the client device; and control a user signaling pattern of the client device based on the calculated context-dependent virtual distance; and a memory coupled to the processor and configured to store related data and instructions. 15. The hub device of claim 14, wherein: the processor is further configured to estimate a physical proximity between the user and the client device; and the processor calculates the context-dependent virtual distance based on both the obtained environmental status information and the estimated physical proximity. 16. The hub device of claim 15, wherein the processor is configured to: estimate a first physical proximity between a first user and a first client device and a second physical proximity between a second user and a second client device within the environment, wherein the first physical proximity and the second physical proximity are substantially equal; and calculate a first virtual distance based on both the obtained environmental status information and the first physical proximity and a second virtual distance based on both the obtained environmental status information and the second physical proximity, wherein the first virtual distance and the second virtual distance are substantially different. 17. The hub device of claim 14, the processor is further configured to calculate the context-dependent virtual distance based on a client device type of the client device. 18. The hub device of claim 14, wherein: the environmental status information is obtained from one or more contextual devices; and the processor is further configured to calculate the context-dependent virtual distance based on a contextual device type of the one or more contextual devices. 19. The hub device of claim 14, wherein: the processor is further configured to predict, based on the calculated context- dependent virtual distance, an answer reaction time of the user in response to a first user signaling pattern of the client device, the processor is further configured to control the user signaling pattern based on the predicted answer reaction time. 20. The hub device of claim 19, wherein: the processor is configured to increase the calculated context-dependent virtual distance based on an expectation that it will be difficult for the user to access the client device; and the processor is configured to increase the predicted answer reaction time based on the increase of the calculated context-dependent virtual distance. 21. The hub device of claim 19, wherein: the processor is configured to increase the calculated context-dependent virtual distance based on an expectation that the user will travel indirectly to the client device to answer a given communication with a given communication type; and the processor is configured to increase the predicted answer reaction time based on the increase of the calculated context-dependent virtual distance. 22. The hub device of claim 19, wherein: the processor is configured to increase the calculated context-dependent virtual distance associated with an interactive video and/or audio interactions relative to non- interactive video, audio, and/or textual interactions; and the processor is configured to increase the predicted answer reaction time with respect to the interactive video and/or audio interactions relative to the non-interactive video, audio, and/or textual interactions. 23. The hub device of claim 14, wherein the hub device is at least one of the client device, another client device in the environment, a remote device that is outside of the environment, or a combination thereof. 24. The hub device of claim 14, wherein the environment is an Internet of Things (IoT) environment. 25. The hub device of claim 14, wherein: the processor is configured to track at least one of (i) interactions between the user and the client device, (ii) changes in the environmental status information, (iii) changes in an estimated physical proximity between the user and the client device, or (iv) a combination thereof; and the processor is configured to calculate the context-dependent virtual distance based on the at least one of (i) the interactions between the user and the client device, (ii) changes in the environmental status information, (iii) changes in the estimated physical proximity between the user and the client device, or (iv) a combination thereof. 26. The hub device of claim 14, wherein the processor is further configured to adjust a power usage pattern based on the calculated context-dependent virtual distance. 27. An apparatus for determining a context-dependent virtual distance based on traced activity, the apparatus comprising: means for obtaining environmental status information relating to an environment in proximity to a client device; means for calculating, based on the obtained environmental status information, the context-dependent virtual distance between the client device and a user of the client device; means for controlling a user signaling pattern of the client device based on the calculated context-dependent virtual distance; and means for storing data and instructions. 28. The apparatus of claim 27, further comprising means for estimating a physical proximity between the user and the client device, wherein means for calculating the context-dependent virtual distance comprises means for calculating the context-dependent virtual distance based on both the obtained environmental status information and the estimated physical proximity. 29. A non-transitory computer-readable medium comprising code, which, when executed by a processor, causes the processor to perform operations for determining a context-dependent virtual distance based on traced activity, the non-transitory computer-readable medium comprising: code for obtaining environmental status information relating to an environment in proximity to a client device; code for based on the obtained environmental status information, the context- dependent virtual distance between the client device and a user of the client device; and code for controlling a user signaling pattern of the client device based on the calculated context-dependent virtual distance. 30. The non-transitory computer-readable medium of claim 29, further comprising code for estimating a physical proximity between the user and the client device, wherein calculating the context-dependent virtual distance comprises calculating the context-dependent virtual distance based on both the obtained environmental status information and the estimated physical proximity.
